content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a placeholder "TODO" (Info.plist), and NSCameraUsageDescription ("This app needs camera access.") is generic and doesn't mention the actual document-scanning feature, plus no location usage is even found in ScannerView.swift's AVCaptureDevice-only permission flow.
Pierre: Apple requires purpose strings that specifically explain why the app needs the permission and ties it to a real, visible feature; a literal "TODO" string will get an app rejected outright, and the camera string is too generic to pass review. Fix by writing a specific description like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and verify whether location is actually used anywhere in the code — if not, remove the key entirely.
